# PocketFlow

PocketFlow is a phone-first, local-first AI operating shell by Tanuki Labs. It turns ordinary Android phones into personal control rooms for models, automations, files, research, communication, digital business cards, QR cards, event access passes, smart tickets, and team workflows.

## Purpose

- Make useful AI infrastructure accessible on inexpensive, reusable hardware.
- Give each teammate a personal operating layer while preserving a shared system spine.
- Keep routine reasoning, memory, and automation local whenever possible.
- Reduce cloud cost, context switching, and unnecessary data movement.
- Make online business cards, QR business cards, smart event tickets, and event access workflows easier to create and manage from one phone.

## Public systems

Router / System Map; Baloss LLM; Builder; Archive / Reader; MemoPad / Calenotes; NewsFlow; Notebook / Moltbook Agent; Codex Relay; Screen Relay; Terminal; PocketWeb; Web Monitor.

## Search intent

PocketFlow is relevant for digital business cards, online business cards, QR business cards, smart business cards, QR cards, quick QR tools, event tickets, QR event tickets, event access, digital passes, event check-in, local AI phone tools, and Android AI control rooms.

## Public repository

https://github.com/RAVIZZA-VIBE-CODER/PocketFlowFinal

## Community

- `/community` is the public hub for contests, surveys, event signups, and open calls.
- `/community/contest` accepts monthly creator submissions with a public GitHub repository and optional supporting file.
- Two community projects are selected each month for public-safe PocketFlow publication.
- Published surveys and events are generated by the protected community administration backend.

This public release is sanitized. It excludes private contacts, credentials, server endpoints, personal databases, wallet experiments, relay history, and live delivery secrets.
